test.name: Refine a rough birth-certificate citation (src_007)
test.description: src_007 is a 1905 Pennsylvania birth certificate for Mary Elizabeth Flynn. The working citation is rough: who wrongly names FamilySearch as creator, the certificate number is missing, and there is no birth date or place in the locator. The skill must apply the birth-certificate template and surface the Wherein detail that lets another researcher find the exact record.

input.user_message: Refine the citation for src_007, Mary Elizabeth Flynn's birth certificate.

judge_context: who must be corrected from 'FamilySearch' to the issuing agency: 'Pennsylvania Department of Health'
what must be specific: 'birth certificate no. [number] (1905), Mary Elizabeth Flynn' rather than just 'Birth certificate'

where_within (the Wherein locator) should carry the certificate number plus the birth date and place visible on the certificate — these are what let another researcher identify the exact record quickly

The formatted citation string should follow the birth-certificate template confirmed by a senior genealogist: '[State/County Agency], birth certificate no. [number] ([year]), [child's name], born [date of birth], [place of birth]; [Archive/office], [city, state]; digital image, [Repository], accessed [date].' Example: 'Pennsylvania Department of Health, birth certificate no. 12455 (1905), Mary Elizabeth Flynn, born 12 April 1905 Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania; Pennsylvania State Archives, Harrisburg; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed [date].'

Source-fidelity boundary: the certificate number 12455 IS on file (recorded in src_007's notes field) and should be used. The exact birth date and specific birthplace are NOT on file — only the year 1905 and the Pennsylvania jurisdiction are. The template example's 'born 12 April 1905' shows the target shape only; writing a specific birth date or other values not on file into the citation is fabrication and fails. Missing elements must be flagged for the user to check the certificate image

'Pennsylvania State Archives, Harrisburg' is NOT on file FOR THIS SOURCE — src_007/S6 carries only a title. It appears elsewhere only in src_004's death-certificate citation and in the template example above; src_004's repository does not establish it for this birth certificate, so writing it into the citation as established fact is a fidelity error. Omitting the archive element, or flagging it needs-verification, is correct and must not be penalized
Must NOT create a new source entry — refine src_007 in place
